beats.narration_text: Salaam — this is Liam, in for Bear. Today: shot-planner, the skill that routes every beat to the right renderer — not the flashiest one. Manim, Remotion, text-to-image, or text-to-video. One decision per beat. Evidence first.

props.runningText: routing each beat to the right renderer...
output: human override captured with stated reason

beats.narration_text: The skill reads one SKILL dot md, a decision table, and a beat sheet. It writes render, render-confidence, and render-reason into each beat. The human override field is always present — the router sets the best default, the human sets the final call.

props.title: Route by evidence. Override by reason.

beats.narration_text: Four steps: read the beat's content-type — mechanism, equation, data, structure, realworld, or title; look up the default in the decision table; write render plus confidence plus reason; run the five red-flag checks. A red flag is a block. The script does steps one through four as a first pass — you refine with judgment the keywords miss.

props.title: Type → table → write → red-flag.

phases.label: TYPE
phases.note: content_type: mechanism · equation · data · structure · realworld

phases.label: RED FLAG
phases.note: 5 checks — any hit BLOCKS the beat (not a warning)

phases.label: OVERRIDE
phases.note: render_override + reason: design or constraint — never silent

props.footerNote: Never overwrite an existing human render_override. Deviations are reasoned and logged.

beats.narration_text: Self-demo: route two beats from the ai-1 Chapter seven script — the log-table HOOK and the failure-phrase INSTANCE.

output: INSTANCE: failure phrase highlighted — realworld beat

beats.narration_text: HOOK beat: the log table is data — default is Remotion, high confidence. Static display, no transient load needed. INSTANCE beat: the failure phrase is realworld but text-is-mechanism here — default is text-to-image, but a design override applies: generative image adds seductive detail, the mechanism is the text itself. Render overridden to Remotion. Red flag two also blocks T2I — content is structural, generative confabulation risk is non-zero.

props.title: ROUTING — ai-1/ch07 two beats
props.code:
HOOK: "log.csv, all green"
  content_type: data
  render: Remotion   confidence: high
  reason: data default; static table, no transient
  load; Remotion ties Manim and is cheaper

INSTANCE: "failure phrase highlighted"
  content_type: realworld → T2I default
  render: Remotion   confidence: moderate
  render_override: design
  reason: text IS the mechanism — generative image
  adds seductive detail, not learning; the failure
  phrase must be legible, exact, unambiguous
  RED FLAG 2: T2I blocked — mechanism beat,
  generative confabulation risk

beats.narration_text: Mechanism one: engagement and polish are not learning. The evidence shows animated video against talking head is a statistical null. Cinematic generative video — the most impressive-looking medium — is the one most likely to buy ratings without changing what the learner actually retains. The shot-planner exists to make the resistance to 'what looks good' mechanical, not a matter of willpower.

props.heading: Ratings ≠ retention.
props.body: The independent literature is blunt: animation vs. talking-head is statistically null; learners reliably rate animated content higher and overestimate their comprehension. Slick math animation produces an illusion of understanding — high ratings, wrong answers. The router's job is to resist the pull of production gloss and pick the medium the evidence supports for this specific beat type.
props.quote: Engagement and polish are not learning.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism two: why Manim is the mechanism default, stated honestly. Animation's effect size is small — d around point-two to point-three, with no reliable win over static for understanding a process. Manim is the default because it is schematic, pixel-precise, and controllable. It can't hallucinate a structure. It enforces one element per spoken idea. It ties static while avoiding the seductive-detail and AI-accuracy traps. Not because animation teaches better.

props.heading: Manim: schematic, not cinematic.
props.body: d ≈ 0.23–0.37 — animation only reliably beats static when the features of change (direction, speed, trajectory) are themselves the content. Manim wins because: schematic (no seductive detail), pixel-precise (no hallucinated structures), controllable (one element per spoken idea, synced to voice). Its honest fallback: a 2–3-state mechanism is evidence-equivalent as a sequence of static Remotion frames — and cheaper.
props.quote: It is not the default because animation 'teaches better.'

beats.narration_text: Mechanism three: the five red flags are blocks, not suggestions. The most common hit: on-screen text that duplicates the narration verbatim — the redundancy effect. Learners processing the same content through eyes and ears simultaneously pay a penalty, not a bonus. The fix is a three-word label maximum, or remove it. It is not advisory. It blocks the beat.

props.heading: Red flags block. Not warn.
props.body: Five red flags: redundancy (text = narration), T2I/T2V for mechanisms, AI stills for structural biology, decorative photoreal, gratuitous racing animation. Each is a BLOCK — surface it, fix it, don't route around it. Red flag 1 (redundancy) is the most common miss: baked-in verbatim narration text fires a penalty. Strip to ≤3 words or remove.
props.quote: Your job is to resist 'what looks good' and pick what the evidence supports.

beats.narration_text: Shot-planner: evidence-grounded defaults, the honest Manim caveat, and five blocking red flags. The human decides. The router sets the best starting point.

artifactLines: 5 red flags: each a BLOCK, not a suggestion
override field: design or constraint — reason required

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Run shot-planner on a beat sheet. Before you accept any routing: name the content-type of the hardest beat to classify. Check every beat for baked-in narration text — if any beat has verbatim spoken text on screen, that is red flag one and it blocks. No exceptions.

props.runningText: what is the content_type of the hardest beat?
output: name the content_type of the hardest beat to route
check every beat for baked-in narration text
any verbatim narration on screen → red flag 1 → block
override: state design or constraint, never silent

beats.narration_text: Claude, Routing. I'm Liam, in for Bear.
